result:
Build is green.

**Report**

- **trace_call location**: `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:2083` (`pub fn trace_call`)
- **What it does**: Thin wrapper that delegates to the existing `crate::debug::trace_call`, converting the C-style `i32` (0/1) trap flag into `bool`. Called from `vm.rs:1404` at the start of each Lua frame execution to fire the call hook when debugging is active.

**Family members also implemented in the same impl block:**
- `trace_exec` (state.rs:2084) â€” wraps `crate::debug::trace_exec(self, pc)`, converts `i32` â†’ `bool`. The natural sibling: same C source area (`ldebug.c`), called paired with `trace_call` in the VM dispatch loop (`vm.rs:1415`).
- `get_proto_instr` (state.rs:2080) â€” implemented as `ci.lua_closure().proto.code[pc]`. Not strictly the trace family, but unblocked because `crate::debug::trace_exec` calls it at `debug.rs:1816`, so trace_exec would have panicked on the first invocation. Changed generic `<T, P>` signature to concrete `(CallInfoIdx, u32)` since the lone call site (`debug.rs:1816`) already passes those types.

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed but not implemented (different families):**
- `dump_proto` (state.rs:2081) â€” proto serializer family, separate ticket.
- `ci_prev_instruction`, `ci_prev2_instruction`, `ci_skip_next_instruction`, `ci_step_pc_back`, `get_ci_pcrel`, `get_ci_vararg_info`, `get_ci_lua_proto_numparams` (state.rs:1538â€“1553) â€” `ci_*` accessor family.
- `CallInfo::is_vararg_func`, `set_recover_status`, `recover_status` (state.rs:312â€“328) â€” CallInfo struct family.
- `get_prev_ci`, `ci_next_func` (state.rs:1491â€“1494) â€” CallInfo navigation family.
- `new_userdata`, `new_c_closure` (state.rs:1644â€“1645) â€” constructor family.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`**: âś… Green (warnings only, no errors).
